macro.headline: Hawkish Fed, sticky energy inflation
macro.body: New Fed Chair Warsh scrapped forward guidance; the dot plot now points to 1–2 rate hikes in 2026, not cuts. With the Iran-war energy shock keeping inflation sticky, long-duration high-multiple growth de-rates first — and the book is tilted there (NOW, ADBE, NVDA, IOT).

risk.detail: FY guide cut (Jun 4): Americas comps −5% (5th straight), tariffs gutted margins, China stalled. No earnings catalyst until Q2.
FY26 growth cut 10.5→7–8.5%; Watchman/EP/urology soft; mgmt guides flat through Q3. The Q2 print is the next test.
Beat & raised (Jun 11) yet sold off; AI-moat fear (Claude Design), CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis. The narrative, not the numbers, drives it.
China export-license tightening + Senate scrutiny; today's AI-spending skepticism is hitting the whole memory/chip complex. Micron's print tonight is a read-through.
No bad news — strong Q1, IBM AI deal. But it's the biggest weight + a high multiple; in a hawkish-Fed software de-rate it leads the drawdown.
Trump MFN pricing deals (Gilead, Novartis signed) + import tariffs put a pricing-pressure overhang on the whole pharma cluster.
FY EPS guide cut on deal financing + a weak flu season; revenue raised. Defensive — a yellow flag, not a red one.

catalysts.why: First big memory read-through since AI-capex doubts flared. A soft outlook would harden the bear case on the whole semiconductor / AI-hardware complex.
An oil spike feeds inflation, keeps the Fed hawkish, and pressures long-duration growth; de-escalation does the reverse. The market's biggest swing factor.
Yields back below 4.5% relieve long-duration growth; a hawkish surprise from Chair Warsh (who scrapped guidance) hits them. The next CPI / jobs prints set the tone.
Most-favored-nation deals (Gilead, Novartis signed) plus import tariffs keep a pricing-pressure overhang on the drug group — a policy story, not a fundamentals one.

catalysts.what: Q2 earnings season — ABT, NOW, BSX
catalysts.why: Guidance is the catalyst: BSX already pre-cut, ABT trimmed EPS, and NOW's read on AI-software demand will set the tape for the high-multiple names.

sectors.note: A bipartisan housing package and falling Treasury yields (oil easing) sent builders sharply higher — the market repricing the prospect of mortgage relief.
Memory and chip names sold off on doubts that hyperscalers' vast AI budgets will earn their keep. Micron's after-close print is the next read.
Diagnostics and life-sciences led gainers as money rotated toward steadier growth — though MFN drug-pricing stays a policy overhang for pharma.

sectors.driver: Oil sliding — Iran is the tail
sectors.note: Crude pulled back, dragging energy and helping yields lower. The Iran conflict is the two-sided wildcard: any supply scare flips this fast.
High-multiple software is squeezed between a higher-for-longer rate path and AI tools encroaching on incumbents' moats.
Banks lean on the yield curve and the Fed's next move; the repricing from cuts toward possible hikes cuts both ways.
